claim_status: no-claim contract inventory only; no ADR-0013, no native adapter prototype, no UI toolkit selection, no trusted-chrome readiness, no accessibility readiness, no page-surface approval, no release-path UI approval, no production claim, and no implementation claim

contract_areas.current_evidence: `crates/turing-ui-model` exposes an immutable `ShellSnapshot`, typed IDs, tab summaries, tab lifecycle state, and validation for duplicate tabs, missing active tabs, and zero snapshot versions.
contract_areas.required_contract: A versioned state contract must define snapshot identity, update ordering, stale-version rejection, coalescing behavior, thread ownership, and which fields are durable product state versus ephemeral toolkit presentation state.
contract_areas.missing_evidence: No accepted ADR-0013, adapter trait, stale-version negative tests, snapshot-update trace schema, or native adapter run proves that toolkit-native state cannot become the product source of truth.

contract_areas.current_evidence: `ShellCommand` records typed intents for tab activation, close, navigation input, tab freeze, tab restore, and Space activation while the UI runtime book requires service-side revalidation.
contract_areas.required_contract: A command contract must define typed command payloads, expected identity and epoch fields, cancellation, deadlines, rejection reasons, confirmation requirements, and service-side validation responsibilities.
contract_areas.missing_evidence: Current commands do not yet carry complete epoch, deadline, cancellation, confirmation, rejection, or trace fields, and no native adapter prototype proves commands cannot bypass kernel or service validation.

contract_areas.current_evidence: `turing-ui-model` exposes a bounded renderer-neutral `PageSurfaceDescriptor` with typed surface, view, document, and device identities, logical/physical dimensions, scale, color/alpha metadata, frame sequencing, recovery state, and one-surface-per-view snapshot validation; the checked page-surface inventory identifies the remaining composition alternatives, failure cases, and blockers for future `UI-GATE-7` work.
contract_areas.required_contract: A surface contract must bind view identity, document epoch, device generation, scale, damage, synchronization, brokered surface handles, fallback frames, and crash or device-loss replacement semantics without exposing toolkit object layouts.
contract_areas.missing_evidence: The descriptor is not an executable page-surface protocol or handle. Brokered handle proof, synchronization, damage, renderer-produced frames, stale-generation receiver rejection, software fallback, latency trace, and `ADR-0016` remain missing.

contract_areas.current_evidence: The checked component fixture and window/input/accessibility inventories name required roles, names, states, page-tree composition, assistive-technology rows, and workflow blockers.
contract_areas.required_contract: An accessibility contract must define native role mapping, names, states, values, focus, live updates, keyboard semantics, page-tree composition, screen-reader support expectations, and platform snapshot evidence.
contract_areas.missing_evidence: No reference-platform accessibility tree snapshots, manual assistive-technology transcripts, page-tree diffs, screen-reader coverage, IME correctness evidence, or `UI-GATE-10` result exists.

contract_areas.current_evidence: The UI runtime testing chapter requires screenshots, accessibility assertions, fault injection, traces, and recovery evidence, but the current M0 crate exposes no diagnostic adapter surface.
contract_areas.required_contract: A diagnostic contract must define trace events, snapshot hashes, screenshot and accessibility capture points, command rejection records, adapter fault injection hooks, crash or GPU-loss observations, and redaction rules.
contract_areas.missing_evidence: No diagnostic schema, adapter trace implementation, fault-injection harness, screenshot baseline, accessibility snapshot, or redaction-reviewed evidence bundle exists.

contract_areas.current_evidence: The UI runtime book requires toolkit types to stay behind Turing-owned state, command, surface, accessibility, and diagnostic contracts, and the M0 model contains no toolkit dependency.
contract_areas.required_contract: An adapter contract must define trait boundaries, event-loop ownership, toolkit object lifetime, update delivery, command emission, resource ownership, error propagation, replacement cost, and package/runtime exclusions.
contract_areas.missing_evidence: No accepted adapter trait, equivalent native adapter prototype, replacement drill, package proof, owner review, or negative test proves that toolkit objects cannot own navigation, profile, permission, credential, agent, Plug-in, persistence, or update authority.

current_model_invariants.still_missing: Adapter update delivery, stale-version rejection, coalescing, trace hashes, and native toolkit binding proof are missing.

current_model_invariants.invariant: typed window profile Space tab and view identities
current_model_invariants.still_missing: Document and device generations now exist in the page-surface descriptor, but process role, principal, origin, frame, and brokered surface-handle identities remain outside the current shell model.
No adapter evidence proves freeze, discard, restore, crash, protected-work, and resource-pressure states are represented consistently across toolkit candidates.
Commands still need epoch, cancellation, deadline, confirmation, rejection, trace, and service-side authority evidence.
Validation currently covers a small shell subset and does not prove adapter update ordering, stale state rejection, accessibility consistency, or surface identity safety.

current_model_invariants.invariant: no toolkit platform GPU page credential Plug-in or agent types
current_model_invariants.still_missing: No native adapter package proof or trait boundary test yet proves that toolkit, page-surface, credential, Plug-in, or agent types stay outside durable product state.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may emit navigation intent but cannot decide URL interpretation, history mutation, origin authority, document epoch, or renderer commit.
required_negative_evidence: stale view navigation is rejected
adapter-supplied URL text is revalidated by the navigation service
page content cannot trigger trusted chrome navigation authority
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: No native adapter negative tests or service-side command rejection trace exists.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may display profile identity but cannot create, switch, migrate, export, delete, or merge profiles or Spaces directly.
required_negative_evidence: cross-profile command is rejected
profile switch requires service validation
adapter state cannot persist profile data
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: Profile, Space, session, and migration schemas are still planning evidence only.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may present permission prompts but cannot grant, persist, expand, or revoke permission authority without permission-service validation.
required_negative_evidence: stale origin prompt is rejected
policy-blocked permission cannot be granted by UI state
default action does not silently allow
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: No prompt adapter, origin epoch binding, policy-lock test, or accessibility transcript exists.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may request credential UI but cannot read, store, autofill, reveal, export, or transmit credentials directly.
required_negative_evidence: credential values never enter adapter traces
autofill requires credential-service authorization
diagnostic capture redacts secret fields
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: No credential UI contract, redaction test, diagnostic schema, or credential-service boundary proof exists.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may present agent observations and confirmations but cannot expand grants, approve consequential actions, or send page data to providers.
required_negative_evidence: model output cannot mint authority
stale document confirmation is rejected
stop and revoke controls remain service-owned
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: No agent confirmation adapter, provider-boundary trace, or consequential-action confirmation evidence exists.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may display Plug-in state but cannot install, update, disable, authorize, or route Plug-in privileges directly.
required_negative_evidence: Plug-in authority cannot be expanded from toolkit callbacks
revocation state is owned by the Plug-in service
untrusted Plug-in UI cannot spoof trusted chrome
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: No Plug-in UI adapter contract, principal model proof, or trusted chrome spoofing test exists.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may show save or restore status but cannot write durable browser state, profile data, session snapshots, settings, or migration outputs directly.
required_negative_evidence: toolkit state is excluded from durable source of truth
settings writes route through owning services
crash restore rejects adapter-only state
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: No persistence boundary test, profile/session schema implementation, or crash-recovery adapter evidence exists.

adapter_authority_denials.denial_rule: The adapter may display update state and commands but cannot choose channels, verify packages, roll back versions, write update metadata, or access signing material.
required_negative_evidence: update command routes through release service
adapter cannot bypass signature or minimum-version checks
no signing or updater secret enters UI traces
adapter_authority_denials.missing_evidence: Research-package and updater-lab records remain planning only and no native update UI adapter evidence exists.

required_evidence.name: ADR-0013
required_evidence.missing_evidence: No accepted ADR-0013 records the UI model, command, state, surface, accessibility, diagnostic, adapter, replacement, and authority boundary decision.

required_evidence.name: state contract
required_evidence.missing_evidence: A complete versioned snapshot/update contract, stale-version rejection behavior, and trace format are not implemented.

required_evidence.name: command contract
required_evidence.missing_evidence: Command epoch, deadline, cancellation, confirmation, rejection, and trace semantics are not complete.

required_evidence.name: surface contract
required_evidence.missing_evidence: Typed page-surface handle, brokered handle, document/device generation, fallback, and latency evidence are not implemented.

required_evidence.name: accessibility contract
required_evidence.missing_evidence: Native roles, names, states, focus, page-tree composition, platform snapshots, and manual assistive-technology evidence are missing.

required_evidence.name: diagnostic contract
required_evidence.missing_evidence: Trace events, snapshot hashes, screenshot capture, accessibility capture, fault injection, and redaction rules are missing.

required_evidence.name: adapter trait contract
required_evidence.missing_evidence: No accepted Rust trait, FFI boundary, or equivalent binding shape exists for toolkit adapters.

required_evidence.name: native adapter prototype
required_evidence.missing_evidence: No Slint, Vizia, Floem, GPUI, or owner-approved reduced reference-shell adapter has run against the shared model.

required_evidence.name: no toolkit-owned authority negative tests
required_evidence.missing_evidence: No negative tests prove toolkit callbacks cannot own navigation, profile, permission, credential, agent, Plug-in, persistence, or update authority.

required_evidence.name: owner review
required_evidence.missing_evidence: UI runtime, accessibility, security, product, release, and architecture owners have not reviewed or accepted this contract.
